title: compare_sign_neighbors_register_matched data payload
description: v0.27.0 sign2vec register-matched diachronic query tool. For a single query sign and a chosen register (divination | magic | literature | auto), returns the top-K nearest neighbors from the (register, NA) and (register, NB) sub-corpus embeddings (same WINDOW=5 / EMBEDDING_DIM=100 hyperparameters, MIN_OCC=20 by default — lowered to 10 for thin buckets). Surfaces the same drift signals as v0.26's compare_sign_neighbors_across_periods PLUS a comparison_with_mixed_register block that quantifies how much of v0.26's mixed-register drift was register-confounded vs how much is residual diachronic signal. Isolates the diachronic axis from the register confound that v0.26 honestly flagged. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

query_sign.description: The sign code submitted by the caller, after whitespace trim.

register.description: The register the comparison was actually run against. When `register='auto'` was requested, this is the auto-selected register. Otherwise it equals the caller's choice.

register_was_auto_selected.description: True iff the caller passed `register='auto'` (or omitted it) and the tool picked the register that best supports the query sign across both periods.

in_na.description: True iff the query sign passes the (register, NA) MIN_OCC gate and has an embedding vector there.

in_nb.description: True iff the query sign passes the (register, NB) MIN_OCC gate and has an embedding vector there.

neighbors_na.description: Top-K nearest neighbors in the (register, Neo-Assyrian) embedding space (empty when in_na=false).

sign.description: Neighbor sign code (e.g. an ABZ identifier).

cosine.description: Cosine similarity between query and neighbor in the (register, NA) embedding. Vectors are L2-normalized at build time so cosine == dot product.

occurrences.description: Occurrence count of this neighbor in the register-filtered Neo-Assyrian sub-corpus.

neighbors_nb.description: Top-K nearest neighbors in the (register, Neo-Babylonian) embedding space (empty when in_nb=false).

cosine.description: Cosine similarity between query and neighbor in the (register, NB) embedding.

occurrences.description: Occurrence count of this neighbor in the register-filtered Neo-Babylonian sub-corpus.

common_neighbors.description: Sign codes appearing in BOTH (register, NA) top-K and (register, NB) top-K. The stable distributional core for this query sign within this register — neighbors that survive the diachronic crossing once register is held constant.

na_only_neighbors.description: Sign codes in (register, NA) top-K but not in (register, NB) top-K. With register held constant, these are stronger candidates for true Neo-Assyrian-period-specific co-occurrence partners.

nb_only_neighbors.description: Sign codes in (register, NB) top-K but not in (register, NA) top-K. With register held constant, these are stronger candidates for true Neo-Babylonian-period-specific co-occurrence partners.

na_only_count.description: Count of NA-only neighbors in this register-matched comparison.

nb_only_count.description: Count of NB-only neighbors in this register-matched comparison.

register_matched_drift_topk.description: Number of top-K positions whose neighbor sign changed between (register, NA) and (register, NB). Equivalent to max(na_only_count, nb_only_count) when both periods returned K neighbors. 0 when the sign is missing from one period — the comparison_with_mixed_register block uses null in that case.

comparison_with_mixed_register.description: Quantifies how much of the v0.26 mixed-register drift signal survives when register is held constant. Positive `drift_attributable_to_register` = register-matching reduced the drift signal (consistent with the v0.26 register-confound hypothesis). null values mean one of the required sources (this register's bucket, or the v0.26 per-period cache) is unavailable for the query sign.

mixed_register_drift_topk.description: v0.26 mixed-register top-K neighbor-list drift for the same sign + same top_k. null if the v0.26 per-period caches are not loaded or the sign is not in BOTH NA and NB mixed-register vocabs.

register_matched_drift_topk.description: v0.27 register-matched top-K drift for this register. null when the sign is missing from one side of the matched bucket.

drift_attributable_to_register.description: mixed_register_drift_topk - register_matched_drift_topk. Positive value = register-matching reduced the drift signal, consistent with the v0.26 register-confound hypothesis. Zero = matching had no effect. Negative = matched drift was higher (small-sample noise dominates the register isolation, or the register IS the diachronic axis for this sign). null if either side is null.

mixed_neighbors_na.description: v0.26 mixed-register Neo-Assyrian top-K neighbors (empty if the sign is not present). Provided for eyeball comparison with neighbors_na.

mixed_neighbors_nb.description: v0.26 mixed-register Neo-Babylonian top-K neighbors (empty if the sign is not present). Provided for eyeball comparison with neighbors_nb.

index_stats.description: Per-(register, period) bucket stats so callers can sanity-check sample sizes and small-bucket MIN_OCC relaxations. Always reports stats for ALL configured registers and BOTH periods, regardless of which register the query targeted.

signs_indexed.description: Number of signs above MIN_OCC in this (register, period) bucket (0 if the cache failed to load).

tablets_in_bucket.description: Number of tablets contributing to this bucket.

min_occurrences.description: The MIN_OCC threshold used for this bucket. 20 by default, lowered to 10 for thin buckets per the v0.26 small-sample heuristic.

loaded.description: True iff this (register, period) cache loaded successfully.
